title: cattleya × claesiana
intro: Cattleya × claesiana, also known as the Clowes' Cattleya, is a hybrid orchid species that is a cross between Cattleya aurantiaca and Cattleya lueddemanniana. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cattleya × claesiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant, making it a potential treatment for bronchitis, coughs, and colds. Cattleya × claesiana is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the plant's flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
